affected_files.change: new file — Compact(graphPath string, log *EdgeLog) (*CSRGraph, error): load existing CSR, enumerate edge-log nodes, merge/aggregate per Decision 1, WriteCSR atomically, then truncate compacted per-node logs.

new file — TestCompaction (+ subtests): repeated ENTITY_COOCCUR weight summing, non-cooccur dedup/last-write-wins, merge with pre-existing graph.dat, empty-log no-op, crash-injection (kill before rename vs after rename/before truncation).
add exported EdgeLog.TruncateNode(sourceFileID uint64) error: closes+drops cached writer, deletes on-disk segment files for that node, so next AppendEdge reopens fresh.
add TestTruncateNode: append, truncate, ReadNode returns nil; append-after-truncate starts a fresh log.
extend EdgeType enum with EdgeEntityCooccur, EdgeLLMAsserted (needed by 3.1.3's own test spec; full validation deferred to 3.1.4).
add 3.1.3 compaction subsection: weight-aggregation semantics, crash-safety ordering, TruncateNode.

dependents: 3.1.4 (engine/graph/edge.go) will build on the EdgeType additions made here.
3.1.5 (traverse.go) and 3.1.6 (graph_test.go) will call Compact()/read graph.dat produced by it.
contracts_touched: graph.dat on-disk format: unchanged (compact.go only ever produces it via existing WriteCSR).
EdgeLog: new exported method TruncateNode; existing AppendEdge/ReadNode/Close unchanged.

EdgeType: two new enum values appended after existing ones (EdgeRedirect=2), preserving on-disk byte values already persisted by 3.1.1/3.1.2 (EdgeTypeInvalid=0, EdgeSplitSibling=1, EdgeRedirect=2 unchanged).
risk_notes: Rewrites the durable adjacency store wholesale; getting the truncate-after-rename ordering wrong would either lose not-yet-compacted edges (if truncated before rename) or silently double-count ENTITY_COOCCUR weights across repeated compactions (if truncation is skipped/retried carelessly). Crash-injection test required per task instructions.
